content: Bid-ask spread is the difference between highest price a buyer will pay (bid) and lowest price a seller will accept (ask). Spread represents transaction cost and liquidity measure: narrow spreads indicate high liquidity and efficient markets; wide spreads signal low liquidity or uncertainty. Spreads widen during volatility, after hours, or for less-traded securities. Market makers profit from spreads while providing liquidity. Limit orders inside the spread can reduce effective transaction costs.
